brent.label: Brent Crude $/bbl
brent.change: +46.5% since Feb 27 — crashed 13.6% to $96.91 on Trump pause, settled ~$104; historic single-day swing
brent.tooltip: Brent Crude: The international benchmark. Pre-war close: $71.00/bbl (Feb 27). Mar 23 (Mon): Crashed from $112.19 to intraday low $96.91 (-13.6%) on Trump's 5-day postponement of power plant strikes, then recovered to settle ~$104 (-7.3% on day). Largest single-day oil price swing of the war. Goldman Sachs raised March-April avg forecast to $110; warns could exceed $147 if Hormuz stays at 5% of normal for 10 weeks. IEA: 'largest supply disruption in history.' Source: ICE Futures Europe / CNBC / Reuters.

wti.label: WTI Crude $/bbl
wti.change: +37.4% since Feb 27 — crashed to $85.66 (-12.8%) on Trump pause, settled ~$91.40; gasoline futures -9%
wti.tooltip: West Texas Intermediate: The US benchmark. Pre-war close: $66.50/bbl (Feb 27). Mar 23 (Mon): Crashed from $98.32 to intraday low $85.66 (-12.8%) on Trump postponement, settled ~$91.40 (-6.9%). Gasoline futures fell 9%, diesel futures -8.5%. Goldman raised March-April Brent avg to $110. Source: NYMEX / CNBC / Reuters.

gas.label: US Gas Avg/Gal
gas.change: +$0.98 / +32.9% since Feb 27 — 23rd consecutive daily increase — highest since Aug 2022 — CA $5.53+
gas.tooltip: National average retail gasoline price. Pre-war: $2.98/gal (Feb 27). AAA Mar 23: $3.96/gal (+32.9% since war began). 23rd consecutive daily increase. Highest since August 2022. Up ~$1.00/gal from a month ago. California $5.53+/gal. Wholesale gasoline futures fell 9% Monday on Trump pause, but retail prices lag wholesale by days. Source: AAA / CNBC / NBC News.

sp500.label: S&P 500
sp500.change: -251 pts / -3.6% — massive relief rally +1.7-2.2% on Trump pause; Dow +960 pts; 445 of 503 advancing
sp500.tooltip: S&P 500 index. Pre-war close: 6,878.88 (Feb 27). Mar 23 (Mon): ~6,628 (+1.7-2.2% on day). Massive relief rally on Trump's 5-day postponement of power plant strikes. Dow gained ~960-1,000 points (+2.1%). Nasdaq +2.3%. Russell 2000 +3%+. 445 of 503 S&P constituents advancing. Defense stocks fell: LMT -1.58%, RTX -1.28%. Energy stocks fell with oil: XOM -1.7%, OXY -4.0%. Gold crashed 6.3% to $4,285.70. Asian markets did NOT benefit: Nikkei -3.5%, Kospi -6.5%. Source: NYSE / CNBC / Reuters.

daily_cost.label: Est. Daily US Cost
daily_cost.note: ~$1.0-1.43B/day — 8,000+ targets — 130+ vessels destroyed — $200B supplemental pending — 5-day pause on power plants
daily_cost.tooltip: Estimated daily operational cost. Day 24: Trump postponed power plant strikes for 5 days, claiming 'productive conversations' with Iran. Strikes continue on all other fronts — IDF struck Tehran, Iran struck Netanya, Gulf states intercepted fresh barrages. 8,000+ targets struck, 8,000+ combat sorties, 130+ naval vessels destroyed. 50,000+ US troops in CENTCOM AOR. CSIS/WSJ estimate $1.0-1.43B/day. $200B supplemental pending. Source: CENTCOM / CSIS / WSJ.

total_cost.label: Est. Total Cost (24 Days)
total_cost.note: Pentagon $11.3B Day 6 + CSIS $16.5B Day 12 + ~$1B/day ongoing — ~$28-34B range — $200B supplemental pending
total_cost.tooltip: Cumulative estimated cost since Feb 28. Pentagon: $11.3B through Day 6. CSIS: $16.5B through Day 12. Extrapolated through Day 24 at ~$1-1.43B/day: ~$28-34B range. No official Day 24 total released. Penn Wharton: $40-95B direct + up to $210B total economic impact. $200B supplemental pending (faces Republican resistance). FY2027 War Dept budget: $1.5T (record). Source: Pentagon / CSIS / Penn Wharton / The Intercept.

targets_struck.label: Targets Struck
targets_struck.note: CENTCOM: 8,000+ US — 8,000+ sorties — 130+ vessels destroyed — ~25,000+ combined est. — strikes continue despite diplomatic pause
targets_struck.tooltip: Total targets struck by US and Israeli forces since Feb 28. CENTCOM (Mar 23): 8,000+ US targets, 8,000+ combat sorties, 130+ naval vessels destroyed. Iran's ballistic missile fire down 90%, drone attacks down 83%. IDF struck Tehran on Mar 23 despite Trump's 5-day pause on power plants. Strikes continue on all non-power-plant targets. Combined US/Israeli extrapolated ~25,000+. Source: CENTCOM / IDF / CNBC.

us_kia.label: US Military Deaths
us_kia.note: 13 KIA hostile + 1 health-related death in Kuwait — ~200 wounded
us_kia.tooltip: US military deaths. 7 by enemy fire: 6 at Port Shuaiba, Kuwait (Day 2), 1 died of wounds at Prince Sultan AB (Day 9). 6 killed in KC-135 crash over Iraq (Day 13, non-hostile). 1 health-related death in Kuwait. Total: 13 hostile + 1 non-hostile = 14. ~200 wounded (vast majority minor; 180+ returned to duty). Source: CENTCOM / AP.

us_wia.label: US Service Members WIA
us_wia.note: ~200 total — vast majority minor — 180+ returned to duty
us_wia.tooltip: US service members wounded/injured across all theaters since Feb 28. ~200 total, vast majority minor. 180+ returned to duty. 50,000+ US service members deployed to CENTCOM AOR. Source: DoD / CENTCOM.

iranian_killed.label: Iranian Killed (Hengaw est.)
iranian_killed.note: Hengaw: 5,900 (595 civ incl. 127 minors) — HRANA: 3,114 — Health Min: 1,500+ — AP: 1,500+
iranian_killed.tooltip: Iranian casualties — significant discrepancies across sources. Hengaw (21-day total): 5,900 killed (595 civilian incl. 127 minors). HRANA: 3,114 deaths (1,354 civ, 1,138 mil). Iran Health Ministry: 1,500+ killed, 18,551 injured. AP (Mar 23): 1,500+ in Iran. Israeli military estimate: 3,000-4,000 soldiers only. 3.9x divergence between Health Ministry and Hengaw. 210+ children killed per Health Ministry. 80,000+ civilian units damaged incl. 300+ health/emergency facilities. Source: Hengaw / HRANA / Iran Health Ministry / AP / Israeli military.

lebanese_killed.label: Lebanese Killed
lebanese_killed.note: 1,029+ killed — 31 healthcare workers — 1M+ displaced — 125K crossed into Syria — 3 Litani bridges destroyed
lebanese_killed.tooltip: Lebanese casualties from Israeli strikes and IDF ground operations since March 2. 1,029+ killed including 31 healthcare workers. 1M+ displaced (~19% of population). 125,000+ crossed from Lebanon into Syria. 472 schools used as shelters. IDF destroyed 3 Litani River bridges — Lebanon's president calls it 'prelude to ground invasion.' Defense Min Katz ordered demolition of border villages. HRW (Mar 23): IDF operations constitute war crimes. Source: Lebanon Health Ministry / OCHA / IDF / HRW.

displaced.label: Displaced Persons
displaced.note: 4.1M+ across Iran, Lebanon, Afghanistan, Pakistan — 3,000+ vessels stranded — 15,000 cruise passengers — UNHCR / OCHA
displaced.tooltip: People displaced from their homes across all fronts. UNHCR: 4.1M+ internally displaced across Afghanistan, Iran, Lebanon, and Pakistan since escalation began. Iran: 3.2M (UNHCR Mar 12). Lebanon: 1M+ (~19% of pop). 125,000+ Lebanese crossed into Syria. 3,000+ vessels stranded (IMO). 600+ in immediate Gulf area. 15,000 cruise passengers on 6+ ships. 40,000+ sailors stranded. Iran internet traffic down 98%. Source: UNHCR / OCHA / IMO.

flights_cancelled.label: Flights Cancelled
flights_cancelled.note: 52,000+ flights — 3,000+ vessels stranded — 40,000+ sailors — 21+ vessel attacks — 7+ seafarer deaths
flights_cancelled.tooltip: Commercial flights cancelled due to airspace closures: 52,000+ since Feb 28 (Travel Tourister/Cirium). 3,000+ vessels stranded in Middle East (IMO via NPR Mar 21). 40,000+ seafarers stranded. ~150 ships anchored outside Hormuz, ~170 containerships (450,000 TEU) trapped inside Gulf. 15,000+ cruise passengers on 6+ ships. 21+ maritime attacks, 7+ seafarer fatalities. JMIC/UKMTO: CRITICAL. Insurance at 5% of vessel value. Source: IMO / IATA / Bloomberg / UKMTO.

children_killed.label: Children Killed
children_killed.note: Iran 210+ (Health Min) + Hengaw 127 minors + Lebanon 111+ + Israel 4 + Kuwait 1 — 1,800+ killed or injured
children_killed.tooltip: Children killed in the conflict. Iran Health Ministry: 210+ children killed. Hengaw: 127 minors among 595 civilian dead. Lebanon: 111+ children. Israel: 4. Kuwait: 1. UNICEF (post-Mar 12): 1,800+ children killed or injured across region. 'One classroom of children every day' — UNICEF. Dimona/Arad Day 22: 19 children hospitalized incl. 12-year-old boy and 5-year-old girl in serious condition. 36 total hospitalized from Dimona/Arad. Source: UNICEF / Iran Health Min / Hengaw / Lebanese MoPH.
